title: malus prunifolia
intro: Malus prunifolia, also known as the Chinese apple, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to East Asia, belonging to the rose family. It has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Malus prunifolia include reducing fever, relieving coughs and sore throats, and aiding in the treatment of digestive issues and diarrhea. The herbal preparation of Malus prunifolia typically involves drying the fruit or leaves, which can be made into teas, infusions, or used in combination with other herbs in traditional remedies.
